key_insight: A polygonal cell-based smoothed finite element framework can model nonlinear geotechnical problems more robustly by smoothing strains over polygonal subcells and avoiding direct shape-function derivatives.
engineering_relevance: The method improves mesh flexibility for complex soil–structure problems, including staged excavation and slope stability, while maintaining accurate displacement, stress, plasticity, and factor-of-safety predictions.
method: Uses Wachspress interpolation on polygonal elements
Applies cell-based strain smoothing with boundary integration over subcells
Implements incremental elasto-plastic updates for Mohr-Coulomb and Duncan-Chang models
evidence_signal: The paper validates the approach on benchmark and engineering cases such as a perforated plate, strip footing, rockfill dam, tunnel excavation, and slope stability, reporting accurate responses and improved efficiency.
watch_item: Implementation in ABAQUS via a user element requires careful handling of polygonal meshes, hanging nodes, and constitutive update stability in strongly nonlinear regimes.
paper_title: Nonlinear Geotechnical Analysis Using a Polygonal Cell-Based Smoothed Finite Element Framework
